Good morning, Fabiana. Thank you for scheduling this call. I understand Bloomberg is looking for a new hardware vendor for the APAC region.
Good morning, Klaus. Yes, I am responsible for procurement and vendor management. We are currently sourcing components for Q4.
Our standard lead time is four weeks. We handle all freight through our logistics partner in Singapore.
That sounds reasonable. Our compliance team will need to carry out a review of the purchase order process. Could you tell me about the turnaround for documentation?
We provide the invoice within 48 hours. If you are looking for an end-to-end contract, we can offer a faster turnaround -- three weeks instead of four.
That is interesting. I would like to look into the end-to-end option. Let me put forward a proposal to my team and get back to you by Friday.
I am responsible for procurement and vendor management at Bloomberg.
Could you tell me about your standard lead time?
If we had known about your services earlier, we would have reached out sooner.
I would like to look into your end-to-end contract options.
Let me put forward a proposal for our next steps.

Good morning, this is Klaus Weber from TechFlow Solutions in Munich. I understand Bloomberg is looking for a new hardware supplier for the APAC region. We specialize in trading terminal components with a lead time of approximately four weeks. Our standard purchase order process includes a compliance review, and we handle all freight arrangements through our logistics partner in Singapore. If you had contacted us earlier about the Q3 order, we would have been able to offer a faster turnaround. I would like to schedule a call to discuss your sourcing requirements in more detail. Could you send me your specifications by end of week?

Hi everyone, quick update on the new vendor onboarding. I have reviewed the invoice from TechFlow Solutions and everything looks compliant. The purchase order was processed yesterday, and customs clearance is expected within five business days. The freight cost came in ten percent below our initial estimate, which is great news. One thing to flag: their standard lead time is four weeks, but Klaus mentioned they could expedite to three weeks if we commit to an end-to-end contract. I recommend we look into this option before the next allocation meeting. If we had known about this earlier, we would have included it in the Q3 forecast. Let me know your thoughts.

Fabiana receives an email from a new vendor in Munich. She reviews the purchase order and checks the compliance requirements. The freight cost is lower than expected. She schedules a call to discuss lead time and sourcing options. They agree on next steps for the end-to-end contract.
